name: Gettysburg 1863 land cover — Oak Ridge / Iverson's field (July 1 Soldier View slice)
note: Separate file from landcover.json BY DESIGN: the committed angle.bundle.json embeds a sha256 of landcover.json, so July 1 features must not touch it (film-safety; ED-21 pin discipline). Geometry basis: no traced-polyline layer exists at Oak Ridge yet — these features are EDITORIAL geometry constructed from the dossier bank's pinned point reads (bachelder-j1-09 drawn bars, wd-iverson-tablet) and the OR reports' descriptions, disclosed per feature and adopted as EDI-1..EDI-2 in docs/reconstruction/iverson-viewpoint-design.md. A sheet-trace hardening pass (crop_sheet.py + georef over j1-09, the Angle-standard method) is the named follow-up before the production render.

features.source: EDI-1: a straight double-faced fieldstone wall through the j1-09 drawn 'BAXTER'S BRIGADE' bar center (4007, 8424), bearing 70° (perpendicular to us-baxter's authored facing 340). Existence and character documented: 'the enemy, strongly posted in woods and behind a concealed stone wall' (or-27-2-iverson); the drawn bar is AT the Mummasburg-road wall (dossier us-i-2-2-baxter EC3.3, drawn=report agreement). Length (470 m) covers Baxter's 392 m authored frontage with margin; straightness is editorial.
features.note: The receiving wall — Baxter's line. The Soldier View death line stands ~90 m NW of it ('advanced to within 100 yards').

features.source: EDI-2: the Oak Ridge crest woods immediately behind (SE of) the wall, 110 m deep band. Existence documented: 'strongly posted in woods and behind a concealed stone wall' (or-27-2-iverson); 'the enemy's strong position in a body of woods, surrounded by a stone fence' (or-27-2-ramseur — the same wall-and-woods Iverson died in front of). Extent is editorial (band depth sized to the viewpoint's viewshed; the j1-09 sheet draws the ridge wooded).
features.note: The woods Baxter's line was posted in; reads as the dark treeline behind the Union muskets from the Confederate line.

features.source: EDI-2: John Forney's open fields NW of the wall — the killing ground. The farm is drawn on j1-09 ('J. FORNEY' at (3614, 8637)); the advance crossed open ground under fire (or-27-2-iverson: the brigade advanced unsupported and its dead lay in an open dress-parade line — or-27-2-rodes corroborating). Boundary geometry is editorial (clipped to the Soldier View crop).
features.note: July grass/pasture surface class; the trampled advance corridor is stamped by the environment bake.
